findings.finding: Child 4 ticket `[rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8]` — which the plan says adds the gate-aware calibrate path this ticket dispatches to — cannot be retrieved from the ticket system (`show_ticket` returns not-found). The CITED-PREREQUISITE ESCAPE therefore cannot be invoked, and the assertion that `calibrate_criterion` will have a code-review-aware dispatch path is unbacked. The current `calibrate_criterion` (lines 413–420 of `eval.py`) unconditionally calls `criterion_prompt_id()`, which always prepends `plan-review-`, producing `plan-review-project-foo` for any `project.foo` id — the code-review solver arm in `eval_solver.run_case` only matches `code-review-<overlay>` prompt IDs and would not handle this case.

findings.finding: The plan's core happy-path dispatch — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 adds to calibrate_criterion' — depends on an unverified capability surface. Today, calibrate_criterion is exclusively plan-review-aware (eval_solver._criterion_id only consults plan_review.registry.by_id; criterion_prompt_id maps every id to plan-review-<id>). Child 4's ticket (rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8) is unfetchable (404), so the concrete API change it will introduce — whether a new `gate=` kwarg, a second function, or a branch inside calibrate_criterion — is unspecified. The CLI dispatch code this ticket writes must know the exact call-site shape to pass a code-review criterion id correctly; without that, the integration cannot be verified as feasible and the dispatch code risks silently routing a code-review id through the plan-review solver (which will produce 'unknown criterion').
findings.location: Approach section — 'dispatch to the gate-aware calibrate path that child 4 [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] adds to calibrate_criterion'; Scope section — src/rebar/_cli/_llm_eval_commands.py

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test case where the criterion id appears in both raw registries but is activated in only one (effective) registry, and assert it is admitted (not ambiguous-rejected), to guard against raw-vs-effective registry confusion.
evidence: The plan specifies `effective_criteria(repo_root)` for the code-review side but does not spell out a test that distinguishes 'present in both raw registries but active in only one' from 'active in both' — the two most important boundary inputs to the ambiguity branch.
Absence: no test scenario for 'registered in both, activated in exactly one' is named in Testing or ACs.
findings.finding: The test plan has no explicit case for a criterion id that is registered in BOTH the plan-review and code-review raw registries but is only ACTIVATED (present in `effective_criteria`) in one of them. This boundary exercises the ambiguity-vs-admission decision path and is the most likely scenario where an implementation bug (checking raw registry presence instead of effective/activated presence) would silently admit or incorrectly reject a criterion.
findings.location: Testing section / Acceptance Criteria — 'Edge: an id activated in BOTH gates is rejected as ambiguous'
scenarios: An id is defined in both plan-review and code-review registries but only the code-review activation is in the project config; without this test, an implementation that checks raw registry presence (instead of effective_criteria) would incorrectly trigger the ambiguity rejection for a valid one-gate case.
findings.suggested_fix: In the synthetic-repo fixture, register `project.foo` in both plan-review and code-review raw registries but only activate it in one project config; assert exit 0 (admitted) not exit 1 (ambiguous).

findings.finding: Child 4 ticket [rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8] does not exist in the tracker, and the 'gate-aware calibrate path' it is asserted to add to `calibrate_criterion` does not exist in the codebase: `calibrate_criterion` always maps via `criterion_prompt_id()` to a `plan-review-*` prompt id, and `eval_solver._criterion_id` only resolves against `plan_review.registry.by_id()`. There is no code-review gate dispatch in the current eval layer.

The plan does not describe how the prompt-id mapping for a code-review criterion will differ, nor does it identify where/how the gate-routing for the prompt id will be introduced.

The decision rule for how a code-review criterion id is translated to a prompt_id and dispatched to the right solver is neither defined in this plan nor co-located with the `calibrate_criterion` signature it modifies.

findings.finding: The physical prompt-id mapping for a code-review project criterion is never defined in this plan or in the codebase. The plan dispatches admitted code-review ids to `calibrate_criterion`'s code-review branch (child rebar:8153-e9b5-5706-49c8), which in turn calls `criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id, gate_key='code_review')` per the child ticket — but `criterion_prompt_id` in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` takes no `gate_key` parameter and unconditionally prepends `PLAN_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX='plan-review-'`. There is no `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX` constant, no `code_review_criterion_prompt_id()` function, and no gate-aware overload anywhere in the codebase. The resolver this plan depends on is referenced but structurally undefined.

findings.suggested_fix: Add `CODE_REVIEW_PROMPT_PREFIX = 'code-review-'` to `src/rebar/llm/criteria/ids.py` and a gate-aware helper `def criterion_prompt_id(criterion_id: str, *, gate_key: str = 'plan_review') -> str` that selects the prefix based on `gate_key`, then reference it explicitly in the Approach section.
